title: Lithocarpus himalaicus
taxonomy:
Lithocarpus himalaicus is classified in the following order: 
It belongs to the kingdom Plantae and is further classified under the subkingdom Angiospermophyta. 
The phylum is Magnoliophyta, and the class is Magnoliopsida. 
The order is Fagales, and the family is Fagaceae. 
This classification indicates that Lithocarpus himalaicus is a species of oak tree in the Fagaceae family.
uses: Lithocarpus himalaicus, also known as Himalayan chestnut, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its bark and leaves are said to have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are believed to help in treating skin infections and reducing swelling. In traditional Ayurvedic medicine, the plant is used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties. The plant's extracts have also been studied for their potential anti-cancer and antioxidant properties. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy of Lithocarpus himalaicus in treating various health conditions.

intro: Lithocarpus himalaicus, commonly known as Himalayan Lithocarpus or Himalayan Bur oak, is a deciduous tree species native to the Himalayan region. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which may aid in treating various ailments such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. Bioactive constituents of Lithocarpus himalaicus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, decoctions, and infusions, which are often marketed as tonics to support immune function and overall well-being.
